Trabalho Prático 1 - ECDB

Brandão et al.

2026-04-19

1 Introdução

O presente trabalho utiliza o conjunto de dados TCGA-PRAD (The Cancer Genome Atlas - Prostate Adenocarcinoma), obtido a partir do Genomic Data Commons, complementado com dados clínicos. Este conjunto de dados é relevante por incluir informação de expressão génica e variáveis clínicas associadas, permitindo comparar tecido tumoral e tecido normal e explorar heterogeneidade tumoral.

2 Objetivos

  • Carregar e pré-processar dados de expressão génica.
  • Integrar dados clínicos.
  • Realizar análise descritiva e estatística univariada.
  • Fazer análise de expressão diferencial.
  • Realizar análise de enriquecimento funcional.
  • Aplicar métodos não supervisionados.

3 Pipeline de análise

  1. Aquisição dos dados com TCGAbiolinks.
  2. Extração da matriz de contagens e metadados.
  3. Filtragem de amostras TP e NT.
  4. Leitura e limpeza do ficheiro clínico.
  5. Filtragem e normalização.
  6. Análise descritiva.
  7. Expressão diferencial.
  8. Enriquecimento funcional.
  9. PCA, MDS, heatmaps e clustering.

4 Fase 1 - Extração e preparação

library(TCGAbiolinks)
library(SummarizedExperiment)
library(edgeR)
library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(cluster)

if (!requireNamespace("pheatmap", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("O pacote 'pheatmap' nao esta instalado.")
}
library(pheatmap)

if (!requireNamespace("clusterProfiler", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("O pacote 'clusterProfiler' nao esta instalado.")
}
if (!requireNamespace("org.Hs.eg.db", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("O pacote 'org.Hs.eg.db' nao esta instalado.")
}
if (!requireNamespace("enrichplot", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop("O pacote 'enrichplot' nao esta instalado.")
}

library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(enrichplot)

set.seed(123)

4.1 Ficheiro clínico

4.2 Aquisição dos dados

query <- GDCquery(
  project = "TCGA-PRAD",
  data.category = "Transcriptome Profiling",
  data.type = "Gene Expression Quantification",
  workflow.type = "STAR - Counts"
)

GDCdownload(query)
dados <- GDCprepare(query)

|=============================================       |88.26715% ~3 s remaining  |=============================================       |88.44765% ~3 s remaining |==============================================      |89.35018% ~3 s remaining  |==============================================      |89.53069% ~3 s remaining  |==============================================      |89.71119% ~3 s remaining  |==============================================      |89.8917% ~3 s remaining   |==============================================      |90.0722% ~3 s remaining   |==============================================      |90.25271% ~3 s remaining  |===============================================     |90.43321% ~3 s remaining  |===============================================     |90.61372% ~3 s remaining  |===============================================     |90.79422% ~3 s remaining  |===============================================     |90.97473% ~3 s remaining  |===============================================     |91.15523% ~3 s remaining  |===============================================     |91.33574% ~2 s remaining  |===============================================     |91.51625% ~2 s remaining  |===============================================     |91.69675% ~2 s remaining |===============================================     |92.23827% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |92.41877% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |92.59928% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |92.77978% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |92.96029% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |93.14079% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |93.3213% ~2 s remaining   |================================================    |93.50181% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |93.68231% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |93.86282% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |94.04332% ~2 s remaining  |================================================    |94.22383% ~2 s remaining  |=================================================   |94.40433% ~2 s remaining  |=================================================   |94.58484% ~2 s remaining  |=================================================   |94.76534% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================   |94.94585% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================   |95.12635% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================   |95.30686% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================   |95.48736% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================   |95.66787% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================   |95.84838% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================   |96.02888% ~1 s remaining  |==================================================  |96.20939% ~1 s remaining  |==================================================  |96.38989% ~1 s remaining  |==================================================  |96.5704% ~1 s remaining   |==================================================  |96.7509% ~1 s remaining   |==================================================  |96.93141% ~1 s remaining  |==================================================  |97.11191% ~1 s remaining  |==================================================  |97.29242% ~1 s remaining  |==================================================  |97.47292% ~1 s remaining |==================================================  |98.01444% ~1 s remaining  |=================================================== |98.19495% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |98.37545% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |98.55596% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |98.73646% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |98.91697% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |99.09747% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |99.27798% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |99.45848% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |99.63899% ~0 s remaining  |=================================================== |99.81949% ~0 s remaining  |====================================================|100% ~0 s remaining       |====================================================|100%                      Completed after 27 s

4.3 Extração dos dados

contagens <- assay(dados, "unstranded")

meta <- as.data.frame(colData(dados))[, c(
  "shortLetterCode",
  "gender",
  "age_at_diagnosis",
  "vital_status"
)]

anot <- data.frame(
  ensembl = rownames(contagens),
  gene_name = rowData(dados)$gene_name,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

meta$sample_id <- rownames(meta)

4.4 Filtragem das amostras

idx <- meta$shortLetterCode %in% c("TP", "NT")
contagens <- contagens[, idx, drop = FALSE]
meta <- meta[idx, , drop = FALSE]

meta$shortLetterCode <- factor(meta$shortLetterCode, levels = c("NT", "TP"))
rownames(meta) <- meta$sample_id

4.5 Leitura do ficheiro clínico

clin <- read.delim(
  "prad_tcga_clinical_data.tsv",
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

clin[clin == "NA"] <- NA

clin <- clin %>%
  rename(
    Patient_ID = `Patient ID`,
    Sample_ID = `Sample ID`,
    Diagnosis_Age = `Diagnosis Age`,
    Disease_Free_Status = `Disease Free Status`,
    Fraction_Genome_Altered = `Fraction Genome Altered`,
    Gleason_score = `Radical Prostatectomy Gleason Score for Prostate Cancer`
  )

clin$Diagnosis_Age <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(clin$Diagnosis_Age))
clin$Gleason_score <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(clin$Gleason_score))
clin$Fraction_Genome_Altered <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(clin$Fraction_Genome_Altered))

clin$Disease_Free_Status <- gsub("^[0-9]+:", "", clin$Disease_Free_Status)
clin$Disease_Free_Status <- recode(
  clin$Disease_Free_Status,
  "DiseaseFree" = "Sem recidiva",
  "Recurred/Progressed" = "Recidiva/progressao",
  .default = clin$Disease_Free_Status
)

4.6 Filtragem de genes pouco expressos

dge_tmp <- DGEList(counts = contagens)
keep <- filterByExpr(dge_tmp, group = meta$shortLetterCode)

contagens <- contagens[keep, , drop = FALSE]
anot <- anot[match(rownames(contagens), anot$ensembl), , drop = FALSE]

rm(dge_tmp, dados)
gc()
##            used (Mb) gc trigger   (Mb)   max used   (Mb)
## Ncells  9173402  490   16623132  887.8   16623132  887.8
## Vcells 24372134  186  854897115 6522.4 1068609247 8152.9

4.7 Normalização

dge <- DGEList(counts = contagens)
dge <- calcNormFactors(dge, method = "TMM")
logcpm <- cpm(dge, log = TRUE, prior.count = 1)

4.8 Sumarização descritiva

table(meta$shortLetterCode)
## 
##  NT  TP 
##  52 501
table(meta$gender)
## 
## male 
##  553
summary(meta$age_at_diagnosis)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.    NA's 
##   15884   20671   22568   22487   24315   28721      53
table(meta$vital_status)
## 
## Alive  Dead 
##   543    10
summary(clin$Diagnosis_Age)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   41.00   56.00   61.00   61.01   66.00   78.00
table(clin$Gleason_score, useNA = "ifany")
## 
##   6   7   8   9  10 
##  45 250  64 138   4
table(clin$Disease_Free_Status, useNA = "ifany")
## 
## Recidiva/progressao        Sem recidiva                <NA> 
##                  92                 403                   6

4.9 Gráficos iniciais

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

barplot(
  table(meta$shortLetterCode),
  col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
  main = "Distribuicao por tipo de amostra",
  ylab = "Numero de amostras",
  las = 1
)

boxplot(
  logcpm[, 1:min(50, ncol(logcpm)), drop = FALSE],
  outline = FALSE,
  las = 2,
  col = "gray80",
  main = "Distribuicao global da expressao",
  ylab = "log-CPM"
)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

4.10 Genes de interesse

genes_interesse <- c("PCA3", "KLK3")

ids_interesse <- sapply(genes_interesse, function(g) {
  x <- anot$ensembl[anot$gene_name == g]
  if (length(x) > 0) x else NA[2]
})

expr_pca3 <- if (!is.na(ids_interesse["PCA3"])) logcpm[ids_interesse["PCA3"], ] else NULL
expr_klk3 <- if (!is.na(ids_interesse["KLK3"])) logcpm[ids_interesse["KLK3"], ] else NULL

if (!is.null(expr_pca3)) {
  dados_pca3 <- data.frame(
    expressao = as.numeric(expr_pca3),
    grupo = meta$shortLetterCode
  )
}

if (!is.null(expr_klk3)) {
  dados_klk3 <- data.frame(
    expressao = as.numeric(expr_klk3),
    grupo = meta$shortLetterCode
  )
}

4.11 Boxplots

par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

if (!is.null(expr_pca3)) {
  boxplot(
    expressao ~ grupo,
    data = dados_pca3,
    col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
    main = "Expressao de PCA3",
    ylab = "log-CPM",
    xlab = "Grupo"
  )
} else {
  plot.new()
  text(0.5, 0.5, "PCA3 nao encontrado")
}

if (!is.null(expr_klk3)) {
  boxplot(
    expressao ~ grupo,
    data = dados_klk3,
    col = c("steelblue", "firebrick"),
    main = "Expressao de KLK3",
    ylab = "log-CPM",
    xlab = "Grupo"
  )
} else {
  plot.new()
  text(0.5, 0.5, "KLK3 nao encontrado")
}

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

4.12 Testes de Wilcoxon

if (!is.null(expr_pca3)) {
  print(wilcox.test(expressao ~ grupo, data = dados_pca3))
}
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  expressao by grupo
## W = 3529, p-value < 2.2e-16
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0
if (!is.null(expr_klk3)) {
  print(wilcox.test(expressao ~ grupo, data = dados_klk3))
}
## 
##  Wilcoxon rank sum test with continuity correction
## 
## data:  expressao by grupo
## W = 8432, p-value = 2.808e-05
## alternative hypothesis: true location shift is not equal to 0

4.13 Expressão diferencial

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = round(contagens),
  colData = meta,
  design = ~ shortLetterCode
)

dds <- DESeq(dds, quiet = TRUE)

res <- results(dds, contrast = c("shortLetterCode", "TP", "NT"))
res <- res[order(res$padj), ]

res_df <- as.data.frame(res)
res_df$ensembl <- rownames(res_df)

res_annot <- merge(res_df, anot, by = "ensembl", all.x = TRUE)

res_annot$significativo <- ifelse(
  !is.na(res_annot$padj) &
    res_annot$padj < 0.05 &
    abs(res_annot$log2FoldChange) > 1,
  "Significativo",
  "Nao significativo"
)

table(res_annot$significativo)
## 
## Nao significativo     Significativo 
##             20741              4545
head(res_annot[order(res_annot$padj), c("ensembl", "gene_name", "log2FoldChange", "padj")], 20)
##                  ensembl gene_name log2FoldChange          padj
## 11396 ENSG00000168389.18    MFSD2A      -6.026735 2.102237e-159
## 14669 ENSG00000188488.14  SERPINA5      -6.810851 1.654719e-152
## 10519 ENSG00000164398.15     ACSL6      -4.970114 2.614136e-138
## 2512  ENSG00000101977.21      MCF2      -5.320128 1.769829e-126
## 1611  ENSG00000085662.14    AKR1B1      -3.931423 1.844825e-125
## 10973 ENSG00000166473.17    PKD1L2      -5.929874 6.428078e-123
## 11782 ENSG00000170370.12      EMX2      -6.823018 1.204120e-118
## 4755   ENSG00000120068.7     HOXB8      -6.322002 1.536775e-117
## 10728 ENSG00000165376.11     CLDN2      -7.966224 4.911162e-115
## 5624  ENSG00000128040.11    SPINK2      -7.470328 6.056039e-110
## 15252 ENSG00000197705.10    KLHL14      -4.289516 5.741519e-105
## 2644  ENSG00000103034.14     NDRG4      -3.406200 1.015720e-104
## 17998  ENSG00000229847.9    EMX2OS      -6.025554 3.877825e-102
## 9567  ENSG00000158955.11     WNT9B      -4.610836  4.461801e-95
## 22784  ENSG00000270885.2   RASL10B      -3.525431  7.406244e-95
## 15053  ENSG00000196844.9     PATE2      -5.487466  2.210005e-94
## 1977  ENSG00000095713.14    CRTAC1      -4.359947  7.007642e-91
## 3517  ENSG00000109667.12    SLC2A9      -2.747776  1.778882e-87
## 7082  ENSG00000137869.15   CYP19A1      -4.641615  1.917550e-86
## 10191 ENSG00000163364.10 LINC01116      -3.602444  1.040294e-84

4.14 Volcano plot

volcano_df <- res_annot %>%
  filter(!is.na(log2FoldChange), !is.na(padj), padj > 0) %>%
  mutate(neglog10_padj = -log10(padj))

ggplot(volcano_df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = neglog10_padj, color = significativo)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Nao significativo" = "gray70", "Significativo" = "red")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Volcano plot - Tumor Primario vs Tecido Normal",
    x = "Log2 Fold Change",
    y = "-Log10 padj",
    color = "Resultado"
  )

Volcano plot - Tumor vs Normal O volcano plot evidencia muitos genes diferencialmente expressos entre tumor e normal, tendo alterações significativas tanto para cima como para baixo, indicando assim mudanças transcricionais profundas associadas ao estado tumoral.

4.15 Gráficos clínicos

ggplot(clin, aes(x = Diagnosis_Age)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribuicao da idade ao diagnostico",
    x = "Idade (anos)",
    y = "Numero de amostras"
  )

ggplot(clin, aes(x = factor(Gleason_score))) +
  geom_bar(fill = "darkorange", color = "white") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribuicao do score de Gleason",
    x = "Score de Gleason",
    y = "Numero de amostras"
  )

clin2 <- clin %>%
  filter(!is.na(Fraction_Genome_Altered), !is.na(Gleason_score)) %>%
  mutate(Gleason = factor(Gleason_score, levels = sort(unique(Gleason_score))))

ggplot(clin2, aes(x = Gleason, y = Fraction_Genome_Altered)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "turquoise3", alpha = 0.8) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Fracao do genoma alterado por score de Gleason",
    x = "Score de Gleason",
    y = "Fraction Genome Altered"
  )

4.16 Enriquecimento funcional

genes_sig <- res_annot %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1, !is.na(gene_name), gene_name != "") %>%
  distinct(gene_name, .keep_all = TRUE)

gene_map <- bitr(
  genes_sig$gene_name,
  fromType = "SYMBOL",
  toType = "ENTREZID",
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db
)

gene_entrez <- unique(gene_map$ENTREZID)
length(gene_entrez)
## [1] 3366
ego <- enrichGO(
  gene = gene_entrez,
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
  keyType = "ENTREZID",
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  qvalueCutoff = 0.2,
  readable = TRUE
)

head(as.data.frame(ego), 15)
##                    ID
## GO:0006936 GO:0006936
## GO:0003012 GO:0003012

## GO:0007389   116
if (!is.null(ego) && nrow(as.data.frame(ego)) > 0) {
  print(
    dotplot(ego, showCategory = 15) +
      ggtitle("Enriquecimento GO - Biological Process")
  )
}

ekegg <- enrichKEGG(
  gene = gene_entrez,
  organism = "hsa",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05
)

head(as.data.frame(ekegg), 15)

## hsa04519 51384/1951/2556/7414/1496/1005/56142/2196/2568/83259/28316/27328/79838/4233/64072/25984/56133/56132/56128/8689/84962/1013/10451/7482/1830/1825/3855/80326/57575/1952/1016/2561/23500/6327/64405/1006/2555/83439/2697/89780/7476/8324/7484/166336/120114/2562/1014/3848/192666/5881/5099/1501/9119/3851/140807/3866/3860/3857/54474/3849/283446/147409/1674/8325/56127/1002/259236/124590/340596/29119/6331/5101/3852/3868/3861/7003/2563/6300/79633/54798/56125/125115/56114/9752/56140/56141/56143/56144/56146/3853/56136/56138/56139/56137/56110/56103/56104/57717
## hsa04820                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   8735/1634/477/2273/7414/1298/8516/10398/6445/1311/3696/9124/6442/4621/2026/1297/7060/3694/2335/6382/4811/11155/8082/8910/2318/29767/482/7137/7140/3691/480/4629/4607/1837/7111/7168/481/3680/287/23500/84675/56776/1465/70/3678/72/6443/10611/25802/3673/165904/171024/6712/1674/137868/9260/1288/4624/7169/1756/3672/4636/89/3690
## hsa05414                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     153/782/489/8516/4842/778/6445/3696/6442/3694/6543/7043/8082/8910/7137/3691/4607/1837/183/7168/3680/776/70/3678/6443/3673/111/1674/859/6546/137868/4624/7169/5350/1756/3672/3690
## hsa05410                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             782/489/8516/4842/778/6445/3696/6442/3694/6543/7043/8082/8910/7137/3691/4607/1837/183/7168/3680/776/70/1636/3678/6443/3673/1674/859/6546/137868/4624/7169/1756/3672/3690
## hsa05412                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              1496/782/489/8516/4842/778/6445/3696/6442/3694/6543/8082/8910/3691/1837/3680/83439/2697/776/3678/6443/3673/1674/859/6546/29119/137868/1756/3672/89/3690
## hsa04082                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      2556/153/1103/6865/5026/2775/2568/9127/11255/1137/6530/6511/5023/3274/60482/116444/148/2914/51764/2791/6538/3351/2774/6531/2561/1813/150/2555/5578/3360/6571/2562/5579/89832/3359/64805/1816/147/136/146/111/1139/5029/3352/1129/5179/2903/1133/6572/2563/22953/116443/2904/151
## hsa04270                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        4638/10335/4660/50487/10398/778/59/5321/26279/148/800/1908/79924/10267/4629/183/185/3779/3708/1909/5578/3778/776/283748/4882/72/4880/5579/94274/147/136/5319/146/111/810/5592/391013/123745/5320/10242/157855
## hsa04972                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       952/22802/477/3784/493/489/6344/8671/1361/1811/1357/50487/760/26279/482/480/9635/481/7220/3708/1360/5578/3778/886/5579/1056/5319/111/5406/885/391013/5320/5645
## hsa05034                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     2775/83933/10645/116444/4852/51764/2791/25759/1644/6531/4915/1813/814/8365/6571/136/111/627/810/8347/2903/653604/8338/317772/8340/399694/8329/8364/8356/8363/116443/8337/440689/8341/55766/2904/8342/8339/8348/8350/8367/3012/8360/8343/8336/8366/8357/8358/8352
## hsa04022                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        477/153/493/4638/10335/489/10398/778/3827/6543/148/3764/482/480/1910/2626/7225/8654/481/185/3779/150/3708/1909/3778/776/4882/4880/624/147/146/111/810/6546/5592/5535/155/10242/4624/5350/100271849/157855/151
## hsa04510                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             3381/7414/1298/5923/4638/4660/5063/8516/10398/57144/1311/3696/858/857/4233/8395/7448/1297/7060/3694/2335/894/5228/63923/25759/3691/10451/1101/1280/3680/56034/5295/5578/3678/3673/2277/5579/5881/80310/596/859/399694/5649/3914/2316/1288/3672/4636/3690
## hsa04024                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 477/153/5733/493/815/489/117/610/10398/778/268/1395/2737/2740/116444/64091/4852/1908/10257/5732/2867/482/7137/64208/480/10451/3351/1081/10021/6751/481/5295/1813/1909/814/6750/776/3360/1816/5881/111/627/810/3352/6752/1129/338442/2903/5350/116443/56670/8843/2904
## hsa04976                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              477/6550/8647/6344/8671/3781/6579/343/366/760/8714/28234/9429/10257/482/480/10599/10864/481/7363/7364/111/123264/7366/10720/54658/54659
##          Count
## hsa04080   100
## hsa04020    73
## hsa04519    98
## hsa04820    64
## hsa05414    37
## hsa05410    35
## hsa05412    31
## hsa04082    54
## hsa04270    41
## hsa04972    33
## hsa05034    49
## hsa04022    43
## hsa04510    49
## hsa04024    53
## hsa04976    27
if (!is.null(ekegg) && nrow(as.data.frame(ekegg)) > 0) {
  print(
    dotplot(ekegg, showCategory = 15) +
      ggtitle("Enriquecimento KEGG")
  )
}

5 Fase 2 - Análises não supervisionadas

5.1 Integração dos metadados clínicos

Para a fase 2 foi feita a integração entre os identificadores das amostras e variáveis clínicas relevantes, de forma a permitir posterior interpretação biológica dos clusters encontrados.

meta2 <- meta
meta2$sample_id <- rownames(meta2)
meta2$patient_id <- substr(meta2$sample_id, 1, 12)
meta2$sample_short_id <- substr(meta2$sample_id, 1, 15)

clin_raw <- read.delim(
  "prad_tcga_clinical_data.tsv",
  sep = "\t",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)

clin_raw[clin_raw == "NA"] <- NA

clin_join <- clin_raw %>%
  transmute(
    patient_id = `Patient ID`,
    sample_id_clin = `Sample ID`,
    sample_short_id = substr(`Sample ID`, 1, 15),
    Diagnosis_Age = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`Diagnosis Age`)),
    Gleason = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`Radical Prostatectomy Gleason Score for Prostate Cancer`)),
    FGA = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(`Fraction Genome Altered`)),
    DFS_status = gsub("^[0-9]+:", "", `Disease Free Status`)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    DFS_status = recode(
      DFS_status,
      "DiseaseFree" = "Sem recidiva",
      "Recurred/Progressed" = "Recidiva/progressao",
      .default = DFS_status
    )
  ) %>%
  distinct(sample_short_id, .keep_all = TRUE)

meta2 <- meta2%>%
  left_join(clin_join, by = c("patient_id", "sample_short_id"))

rownames(meta2) <- meta2$sample_id

5.2 Transformação VST

dds_unsup <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = round(contagens),
  colData = meta,
  design = ~ shortLetterCode
)

dds_unsup <- estimateSizeFactors(dds_unsup)
vst_mat <- assay(vst(dds_unsup, blind = TRUE))

5.3 Anotação dos genes

gene_symbols <- anot$gene_name[match(rownames(vst_mat), anot$ensembl)]
gene_symbols <- as.character(gene_symbols)
gene_symbols[is.na(gene_symbols) | gene_symbols == ""] <- rownames(vst_mat)[is.na(gene_symbols) | gene_symbols == ""]
rownames(vst_mat) <- make.unique(gene_symbols)

5.4 Seleção dos genes mais variáveis

gene_var_all <- apply(vst_mat, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
gene_var_all[is.na(gene_var_all)] <- 0

top500_all <- names(sort(gene_var_all, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(500, length(gene_var_all))]
top50_all <- names(sort(gene_var_all, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(50, length(gene_var_all))]

mat_pca_all <- vst_mat[top500_all, , drop = FALSE]
mat_heat_all <- vst_mat[top50_all, , drop = FALSE]

mat_pca_all <- mat_pca_all[
  apply(mat_pca_all, 1, function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0 && all(is.finite(x))),
  , drop = FALSE
]

mat_heat_all <- mat_heat_all[
  apply(mat_heat_all, 1, function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0 && all(is.finite(x))),
  , drop = FALSE
]

if (nrow(mat_pca_all) < 2 || ncol(mat_pca_all) < 2) {
  stop("Nao ha genes/amostras suficientes para PCA global.")
}

5.5 PCA global

# PCA - todas as amostras
pca_all <- prcomp(t(mat_pca_all), scale. = TRUE)

pca_all_df <- data.frame(
  sample_id = rownames(pca_all$x),
  PC1 = pca_all$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca_all$x[, 2]
) %>%
  left_join(meta2, by = "sample_id")

var_exp <- summary(pca_all)$importance[2, 1:2] * 100

ggplot(pca_all_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = shortLetterCode)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA - todas as amostras",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_exp[1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_exp[2], 1), "%)"),
    color = "Grupo"
  )

PCA - Todas as amostras O PCA demonstra uma separação clara entre o tecido normal e tumoral. As amostras NT agrupam-se de forma mais homogénea, enquanto os tumores são mais dispersos, refletindo assim maior variabilidade biológica

5.6 MDS global

dist_all <- dist(t(mat_pca_all))
mds_all <- cmdscale(dist_all, k = 2)

mds_all_df <- data.frame(
  sample_id = rownames(mds_all),
  Dim1 = mds_all[, 1],
  Dim2 = mds_all[, 2]
) %>%
  left_join(meta2, by = "sample_id")

ggplot(mds_all_df, aes(x = Dim1, y = Dim2, color = shortLetterCode)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "MDS - todas as amostras",
    x = "Dimensao 1",
    y = "Dimensao 2",
    color = "Grupo"
  )

MDS – Todas as amostras O MDS confirma o padrão do PCA: NT e TP formam dois grupos distintos. Os tumores apresentam maior espalhamento, sugerindo subtipos ou diferenças clínicas internas.

5.7 Heatmap global

mat_heat_all_z <- t(scale(t(mat_heat_all)))
mat_heat_all_z[!is.finite(mat_heat_all_z)] <- 0

ann_all <- meta2[, c("shortLetterCode"), drop = FALSE]
rownames(ann_all) <- meta2$sample_id
ann_all <- ann_all[colnames(mat_heat_all_z), , drop = FALSE]

pheatmap(
  mat_heat_all_z,
  annotation_col = ann_all,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  fontsize_row = 7,
  main = "Heatmap - 50 genes mais variaveis"
)

Heatmap – Genes diferencialmente expressos O heatmap separa nitidamente NT e TP, mostrando blocos de genes co‑regulados. Vários genes conhecidos no contexto tumoral surgem com padrões característicos, validando a análise.

5.8 Análise dos Tumores

idx_tumor <- meta2$shortLetterCode == "TP"
vst_tumor <- vst_mat[, idx_tumor, drop = FALSE]
meta_tumor <- meta2[idx_tumor, , drop = FALSE]

if (ncol(vst_tumor) < 3) {
  stop("Poucas amostras tumorais para clustering.")
}

gene_var_tumor <- apply(vst_tumor, 1, var, na.rm = TRUE)
gene_var_tumor[is.na(gene_var_tumor)] <- 0

top500_tumor <- names(sort(gene_var_tumor, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(500, length(gene_var_tumor))]
top50_tumor <- names(sort(gene_var_tumor, decreasing = TRUE))[1:min(50, length(gene_var_tumor))]

mat_pca_tumor <- vst_tumor[top500_tumor, , drop = FALSE]
mat_pca_tumor <- mat_pca_tumor[
  apply(mat_pca_tumor, 1, function(x) var(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0 && all(is.finite(x))),
  , drop = FALSE
]

if (nrow(mat_pca_tumor) < 2 || ncol(mat_pca_tumor) < 3) {
  stop("Poucos genes/amostras validos para clustering tumoral.")
}

5.9 PCA tumoral e clustering

pca_tumor <- prcomp(t(mat_pca_tumor), scale. = TRUE)
pc_scores <- pca_tumor$x[, 1:min(10, ncol(pca_tumor$x)), drop = FALSE]
ks <- 2:min(6, nrow(pc_scores) - 1)

sil_means <- sapply(ks, function(k) {
  km <- kmeans(pc_scores, centers = k, nstart = 25)
  ss <- silhouette(km$cluster, dist(pc_scores))
  mean(ss[, 3])
})

best_k <- ks[which.max(sil_means)]
km_final <- kmeans(pc_scores, centers = best_k, nstart = 50)

meta_tumor$cluster <- factor(km_final$cluster)

sil_df <- data.frame(
  k = ks,
  silhouette_media = sil_means
)

sil_df
##   k silhouette_media
## 1 2        0.1669893
## 2 3        0.1708331
## 3 4        0.1598964
## 4 5        0.1664345
## 5 6        0.1514964

5.10 PCA tumoral colorido por cluster

pca_tumor_df <- data.frame(
  sample_id = rownames(pca_tumor$x),
  PC1 = pca_tumor$x[, 1],
  PC2 = pca_tumor$x[, 2]
) %>%
  left_join(meta_tumor, by = "sample_id")

var_exp_t <- summary(pca_tumor)$importance[2, 1:2] * 100

ggplot(pca_tumor_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = paste0("PCA - tumores apenas (k = ", best_k, ")"),
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_exp_t[1], 1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_exp_t[2], 1), "%)"),
    color = "Cluster"
  )

PCA – Tumores apenas (k = 3) Ao analisar apenas tumores, surgem três clusters distintos. Isto indica-nos que a heterogeneidade tumoral é estruturada e não aleatória, sugerindo subtipos moleculares.

5.11 Heatmap tumoral

mat_top50_tumor <- vst_tumor[top50_tumor, , drop = FALSE]
mat_top50_tumor_z <- t(scale(t(mat_top50_tumor)))
mat_top50_tumor_z[!is.finite(mat_top50_tumor_z)] <- 0

ann_tumor <- meta_tumor[, c("cluster", "Gleason", "DFS_status"), drop = FALSE]
rownames(ann_tumor) <- meta_tumor$sample_id
ann_tumor <- ann_tumor[colnames(mat_top50_tumor_z), , drop = FALSE]

pheatmap(
  mat_top50_tumor_z,
  annotation_col = ann_tumor,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  fontsize_row = 7,
  main = "Heatmap dos 50 genes mais variaveis nos tumores"
)

Heatmap – 50 genes mais variáveis nos tumores Este heatmap mostra-nos que os padrões de expressão dos genes mais variáveis se alinham parcialmente com os clusters tumorais e com características clínicas como Gleason e recidiva, sugerindo relevância biológica e potencial valor prognóstico.

5.12 Relação exploratória com clínica

print(table(Cluster = meta_tumor$cluster, Gleason = meta_tumor$Gleason, useNA = "ifany"))
##        Gleason
## Cluster   6   7   8   9  10
##       1  19 109  28  41   1
##       2  13  79  23  57   0
##       3  14  61  14  39   3
print(table(Cluster = meta_tumor$cluster, DFS = meta_tumor$DFS_status, useNA = "ifany"))
##        DFS
## Cluster Recidiva/progressao Sem recidiva <NA>
##       1                  26          170    2
##       2                  36          134    2
##       3                  29          100    2
meta_tumor_fga <- meta_tumor[!is.na(meta_tumor$cluster) & !is.na(meta_tumor$FGA), , drop = FALSE]

if (nrow(meta_tumor_fga) > 0) {
  print(aggregate(FGA ~ cluster, data = meta_tumor_fga, summary))
}
##   cluster   FGA.Min. FGA.1st Qu. FGA.Median   FGA.Mean FGA.3rd Qu.   FGA.Max.
## 1       1 0.00000000  0.00020000 0.02745000 0.06230306  0.08567500 0.53790000
## 2       2 0.00000000  0.04435000 0.07090000 0.09963684  0.13015000 0.57820000
## 3       3 0.00000000  0.03550000 0.08565000 0.12145156  0.17000000 0.46450000
if (nrow(meta_tumor_fga) > 0 && length(unique(meta_tumor_fga$cluster)) > 1) {
  print(kruskal.test(FGA ~ cluster, data = meta_tumor_fga))
}
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  FGA by cluster
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 59.386, df = 2, p-value = 1.272e-13

5.13 Gráfico de silhouette

ggplot(sil_df, aes(x = k, y = silhouette_media)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Escolha do numero de clusters",
    x = "k",
    y = "Silhouette media"
  )

## Interpretação da fase 2

As análises não supervisionadas permitiram-nos observar a estrutura global dos dados sem impor grupos de antemão. Em particular, PCA e MDS ajudam-nos a perceber se as amostras tumorais e normais apresentam separação global consistente.

O heatmap dos genes mais variáveis ajuda nesta leitura pois destaca padrões de coexpressão e possíveis blocos de amostras com comportamento semelhante. Quando a organização das amostras acompanha o tipo de tecido, isso acaba por reforçar a presença de diferenças transcriptómicas robustas.

A análise restrita dos tumores permite-nos examinar a heterogeneidade interna do grupo tumoral. O clustering por K-means, apoiada pela métrica de silhouette, constitui uma análise preliminar útil para a identificação de subgrupos potencialmente associados a variáveis clínicas como o Gleason score, o estado livre de doença ou a fração do genoma alterado.

6 Discussão geral

Os resultados que obtivemos demonstram que o conjunto TCGA-PRAD é sim adequado para análise integrada de expressão génica e metadados clínicos. Ao comparar tecido tumoral com o tecido normal podemos identificar genes diferencialmente expressos e também explorar alguns marcadores de interesse reconhecidos no contexto do cancro da próstata.

A integração da análise de enriquecimento funcional faz com que a interpretação dos resultados se torne mais robusta, ao permitir identificar processos biológicos e vias moleculares sobre-representadas entre os genes alterados. Desta forma, a análise passa de se centrar apenas em genes isolados para agora considerar mecanismos biológicos mais amplos.

As análises não supervisionadas demonstram ainda que existem estruturas nos dados e possivel heterogeneidade dentro do conjunto tumoral. Apesar destas abordagens serem exploratórias, estas constituem uma base importante para etapas futuras de modelação preditiva e seleção de genes.

7 Conclusão

Durante o trabalho desenvolvemos um pipeline reprodutível em R/Bioconductor para a análise de dados de expressão génica no conjunto TCGA-PRAD. Realizamos tarefas de preparação e pré-processamento, análise descritiva, testes univariados, análise de expressão diferencial, enriquecimento funcional e análises não supervisionadas.

No contexto global os resultados sugerem diferenças transcriptómicas relevantes entre tumor primário e tecido normal, bem como heterogeneidade entre tumores. os resultados obtidos sustentam a continuação do trabalho para a próxima fase, sendo essa centrada e análise preditiva e seleção de genes.
